figures.caption: Table 37: Selector performance under alternative oracle-knee tolerances.
Table 36: Case-level oracle-knee locations under alternative near-optimality tolerances.
Table 40: Case-level realized timing for the principal search baselines and the exhaustive reference.
Table 23: Anchor-source conditions used to test robustness of the clean-system stage. The downstream selector is unchanged across rows.
Table 25: Aggregate downstream-selection quality under noisy, partial, and same-data anchor estimation.

Table 27: Case-level SAKE selections under representative robustness conditions. Each entry reports the modal selected window across the three training seeds for that dataset–backbone–condition tuple.
Table 26: Case-level anchor pairs under representative robustness conditions. Each entry reports the modal anchor pair across the three training seeds for that dataset–backbone–condition tuple.

Table 30: Aggregate selector quality under proxy-anchor extraction. Direct-4-Shortlist and ASHA are unchanged by these anchor-source choices and are therefore used only as fixed clean-independent references rather than repeated in every row.

Table 19: Shared training and evaluation protocol for all four backbones. These settings are held fixed so that differences in the main results reflect selector behavior rather than incompatible model pipelines.
Table 35: Aggregate sensitivity of SAKE to the fixed selector constants.
